title: Indicator (long-form fact table)
description: A single socio-economic, demographic, infrastructure, or fiscal indicator stored as long-form rows: (entity_kind, entity_id, time, value, unit, ...). Joined to geometry at view time via the existing identifier conventions (ECI state codes, LGD district codes). Indicators carry semantic hints (`value_kind`, `direction`) so the frontend's generic chart components can render them without per-indicator code.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Long-form indicator with declared time_grain, value_kind, direction, unit. Created for the socio-economic expansion (TODO/SOCIO-ECONOMIC-EXPANSION.md, decisions D1/D4/Q3, locked 2026-05-10).

Additive: optional governance/honesty metadata on indicator block. Added attribution_geography, comparability, funding_split, implementing_authority, methodology_vintage, series_breaks, icon. Driven by Governance Strategist review 2026-05-11 — the v1.0 schema let an analyst publish a ranked-choropleth indicator without declaring whether the values are even comparable across states. The new fields force honest framing without breaking any existing artifact (all optional).

Additive: optional render-hint fields on indicator block. Added chart_type (enum: choropleth | ranked | stacked-trend) so a facetted indicator can declare its preferred default primitive without per-id branching in the frontend; added default_mode (enum: absolute | percent) for stacked/ranked views where percent vs absolute is a real editorial choice. Both optional — existing v1.1 artifacts remain valid. Driven by ADR-0024 (backend Aggregator for facetted indicators) and the StackedTrend chart primitive landing in the same wave.

Additive: optional row-level `vintage` field. Carries the upstream's revision-tier label per row (e.g. 'First Advance Estimates', 'Provisional Estimates', 'First Revised Estimates', 'Final Estimates') so the chart can honestly surface that recent FY values may revise. Driven by the MoSPI National Accounts Statistics ingest (2026-05-14): the source publishes the same (indicator, year) pair in multiple revision tiers; we pick the most-final per (indicator, year) and stamp the chosen vintage on each row. Optional — existing v1.2 artifacts remain valid.

Additive: optional `indicator.facet_labels` map. Lets a facetted indicator (chart_type = stacked-trend, rows[].facet populated) declare the human-readable label per facet value (e.g. {coal: 'Coal', other_thermal: 'Other thermal'}). The frontend stops carrying topic-level hardcoded label literals and reads the labels from the artifact (composer-as-source-of-truth), with a humanise(facet_id) fallback when the field is absent so old artifacts keep rendering. Driven by Phase 4 C2 of TODO/VIZ-LAYER-GAPS-PLAN.md (lift facet labels into the schema). Optional — existing v1.3 artifacts remain valid.

Additive (Hans governance fields per TODO/PER-INDICATOR-DOCS-PLAN.md). (a) `indicator.comparability` enum extended with the 4-level honest ladder: `comparable_across_states_and_time`, `comparable_across_states_snapshot_only`, `comparable_within_state_over_time`, `directional_only`. The old 3 tokens remain valid (deprecated, mapping rule: `comparable_across_states` → `comparable_across_states_and_time`; `comparable_with_normalisation` → splits per artifact into level 2 / 3 / 4 on next touch; `not_comparable_across_states` → `directional_only`). (b) `indicator.denominator` accepts an object form `{what, price_basis, base_year, source_artifact}` in addition to the legacy string form so ratio indicators can declare their normalisation surface; both shapes remain valid. (c) Three new optional indicator fields: `revision_tier_by_period[]` (array of `{from, tier, note?}` so a fiscal artifact can declare per-period RE/BE/A vintage), `excludes[]` (array of citizen-facing what's-NOT-counted bullets, e.g. 'IGNOAPS social pensions'), `renderer_rules[]` (array of slug strings from a controlled vocabulary, e.g. 'no_growth_across_break'). All v1.4 artifacts remain valid; backfill is opt-in per artifact.

Expand phase for the folded indicator model (TODO/20260517-folded-indicator-and-collection-inventory-handover.md). Adds four optional top-level blocks — `methodology`, `series_spec`, `collection_inventory`, `divergence` — plus optional `rows[].period_label` (adapter-owned citizen label matching the period token; existing `rows[].time` remains the machine sort/join key). Adds the optional `$ref` form for `series_spec.expected_geographies` that targets `datasets/reference/in/universes.json`. All v1.5 fields and behaviours remain valid; the contraction phase that makes the new blocks required and deletes the indicator-notes sidecar lands in v2.0. Strict `$schema_version == x-version` enforcement is unchanged; existing 106 indicators are mechanically bumped to `$schema_version: 1.6` in the same commit, no other content change.

Contract phase for the folded indicator model. The four blocks introduced as optional in v1.6 — `methodology`, `series_spec`, `collection_inventory`, `divergence` — are now `required`. The companion `indicator-notes.schema.json` sidecar contract is deleted in this commit; the 10 `.notes.json` sidecars under `datasets/indicators/in/` were folded losslessly into the inline `methodology` block in the preceding commit. `divergence` remains a reserved `null` slot until the divergence-reporting subsystem ships. All 110 existing indicators are mechanically bumped to `$schema_version: 2.0` in the same commit (see `tools/bump_indicators_to_2_0.py`); their content is unchanged because the fold migration already populated the new blocks. Per the working contract: schema bumps remain breaking — there is no `additionalProperties: true` escape hatch, and `$schema_version` MUST equal `x-version`.

Narrowed constraint: `series_spec.expected_geographies` is inline-array-only; the `{"$ref": "universes.json#/universes/<key>"}` form added in v1.6 is removed. The companion `datasets/reference/in/universes.json` reference file and `datasets/schemas/universes.schema.json` are deleted in this commit because they had zero consumers — every existing indicator's `expected_geographies` was auto-seeded inline by the v1.6 fold migration, so dropping the indirection is a no-op for current data. Rationale: a level of indirection that no artifact uses is dead weight; each indicator declares its own geographic universe inline so the contract surface stays self-contained. All 110 indicators are mechanically bumped to `$schema_version: 3.0` via `tools/bump_indicator_schema_to_current.py`; no content change. Per the working contract: narrowing a constraint is MAJOR (CLAUDE.md §11) even when no on-disk artifact would have failed under the new shape.

Inventory lift (TODO/20260517 §16). Removed block `collection_inventory` and removed sub-fields `series_spec.expected_geographies`, `series_spec.expected_periods`, `series_spec.expected_periods_inference`. Status / observed periods / observed geographies / unavailable periods / last_attempt now live in the external index `datasets/reference/in/indicators-completeness.json` (its own schema, v2.0). Operator-set flags (`frozen`, `refetch_requested`, `unavailable_periods`) move to the operator overlay `datasets/reference/in/indicators-operator-state.json` (its own schema, v1.0). Rationale: ADR-0026 — derived/operational state is operator-axis, not citizen-axis; folding it into the citizen-facing artifact violated the separation and caused the 2026-05-16 fetched_at smear (every re-derive of inventory pushed `last_collected_at` into every artifact). The 110 indicator artifacts retain only the citizen-axis surface: `series_spec` shrinks to `{description}` only; everything else is unchanged. Mechanical migration via `tools/rip_to_v4.py`.

Additive: optional `indicator.cadence` field declaring the publisher's release cadence, distinct from `indicator.time_grain` (which describes per-row time-token resolution). Enum: annual_cy | annual_fy | quarterly_cy | quarterly_fy | monthly | weekly | daily | decennial | ad_hoc. Per ADR-0027. Driven by the Phase #1 spike of TODO/20260517-coverage-temporal-range-plan.md, which surfaced eight artifacts whose `time_grain=year` falsely implied annual cadence (Census x2, BUR-GHG x2, HDI, etc.) and would otherwise have produced citizen-misleading 'gap_count' captions. The companion `derive_temporal_range()` reads this field and omits `gap_count_within_range`/`observed_periods_within_range` for `decennial`/`ad_hoc`. Optional — existing v4.0 artifacts remain valid; the four unambiguous witnesses (Census x2, BUR-GHG x2) are retagged in the same commit. The paired TypeScript union in `frontend/src/lib/indicators.ts` is widened in the same commit per /memories/lessons.md 2026-05-17 #1.

Additive: extend `indicator.attribution_geography` enum with `where_allocated` for central-sector / pooled-allocation series where the value represents a state's allocated SHARE of an asset that is physically sited elsewhere. Driven by the CEA Monthly Executive Summary IC sheet: every state receives a non-zero nuclear / hydro / coal allocation via the regional grid even when no plant is sited inside its boundary, so the existing `where_produced` value (which triggers a 'Values reflect siting' banner) was actively misleading on the all-India choropleth (J&K showed 95 MW with a 'where the asset is located' caveat). The 8 CEA `installed_capacity_*` artifacts under `datasets/indicators/in/energy/` are retagged to `where_allocated` in the same commit. Renderer behaviour: `IndicatorChoropleth` and `StackedTrend` surface a tailored banner — 'Values are this state's allocated share of central-sector capacity, not the location of the plant.' The paired TypeScript union in `frontend/src/lib/indicators.ts` and the Zod enum in `frontend/src/lib/charts/stacked-trend/types.ts` are widened in the same Tier-A commit per /memories/lessons.md 2026-05-16 #1. All v4.1 artifacts remain valid; the 110 indicator artifacts are mechanically bumped to `$schema_version: 4.2` via `tools/bump_indicator_schema_to_current.py`.

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002, extended per D3 with optional name + authority for human attribution.

indicator.description: Identity and rendering metadata for the indicator. The four hint fields (value_kind, direction, scale_hint, unit) drive the generic chart components — every indicator is renderable without per-indicator UI code (D1, design-system principle).

id.description: Stable namespaced id (e.g. "energy/installed_mw_by_state", "economy/gsdp_constant_prices_2011_12"). Used for cross-references and URL slugs.

title.description: Human-readable title shown in legends and headings.

description.description: One-paragraph definition. Citizen-readable — avoid jargon, or translate it.

entity_kind.description: What the indicator is measured per. Joins to the matching identifier convention (ECI state, LGD district, etc.).

time_grain.description: Resolution of the `time` field on each row. `year`/`fiscal_year` → YYYY; `quarter`/`month` → YYYY-MM; `date` → YYYY-MM-DD. Per Q3 (TODO/SOCIO-ECONOMIC-EXPANSION.md). DISTINCT FROM `cadence`: `time_grain` is the stamp resolution of one row; `cadence` is how often the publisher releases new rows. See ADR-0027.

cadence.description: Optional (v4.1+). Publisher's release cadence — how often a new observation is published. DISTINCT FROM `time_grain` (which is the resolution of one row's `time` token). Examples: Census is decennial (cadence=decennial, time_grain=year, token='1961'); RBI quarterly statements are quarterly_fy with monthly stamps (cadence=quarterly_fy, time_grain=quarter, token='2024-04'); UNFCCC GHG inventory is ad-hoc. `decennial`/`ad_hoc` series have no defined inter-observation interval; the `derive_temporal_range()` consumer omits gap_count/observed_periods for these because asserting them would mislead. When absent, the consumer falls back to inferring cadence from `time_grain`. Per ADR-0027.

value_kind.description: Drives formatting and choice of scale. `count` → integer; `rate` → per-unit (e.g. per capita); `share` → 0..1; `currency` → with `unit`; `index` → unitless ratio; `duration` → with `unit` (days, hours); `raw` → no formatter (escape hatch).

direction.description: Drives the colour ramp choice. `higher_is_better` → sequential warm; `lower_is_better` → sequential warm reversed; `neutral` → sequential cool. Diverging ramps are reserved for explicit comparison views and are not chosen automatically.

scale_hint.description: Suggested d3 scale type; the chart may override if the data demands.

unit.description: Unit string (e.g. "MW", "INR (crore)", "per 100k", "%", "years"). For `share`, use "%" or "share" to disambiguate display.

denominator.description: When this indicator is itself a derived per-X rate, declares the normalisation surface. v1.0–v1.4 string form (the indicator id of the denominator, e.g. "people/population_total") remains valid. v1.5 object form lets a ratio against a non-indicator denominator (e.g. GSDP at current prices, base 2011-12) be declared without forging a sibling indicator first. `null` for raw indicators.

notes.description: Caveats the citizen should know (base year, methodology change, sample frame). Surfaced in the chart's source card.

icon.description: Optional icon hint (lucide-svelte name or path under frontend/src/lib/icons/indicators/). The chart shows it next to the title to make the indicator scannable in lists. When absent, a generic dot is used. Examples: "zap" (energy), "factory" (manufacturing), "heart-pulse" (health), "book-open" (education).

attribution_geography.description: What the entity_id geographically represents. `where_produced`: asset is physically sited in this state (e.g. nameplate of plants whose coordinates fall inside the state). `where_allocated`: state's allocated SHARE of a centrally-pooled asset that is sited elsewhere (e.g. CEA's per-state nuclear / central-sector capacity flowing back via the regional grid — every state shows non-zero even with no plant inside its boundary). `where_consumed`: end-use happens in this state (e.g. per-capita electricity consumption). `where_billed`: financial/billing residence (e.g. GST collection). `where_resident`: persons resident in the state (e.g. population, NFHS). `where_administered`: administrative jurisdiction regardless of where the activity happens (e.g. cases filed in state HC). When this is `where_produced`, `where_allocated`, or `where_billed`, the chart MUST surface a citizen-facing banner clarifying that the map shows siting / allocation / billing, not service. Per Governance review 2026-05-11; `where_allocated` added v4.2 (2026-05-17).

comparability.description: Honest declaration of whether ranking states by this indicator's raw value is meaningful. v1.5 4-level ladder (preferred): `comparable_across_states_and_time` (rank states AND trace trends), `comparable_across_states_snapshot_only` (rank states today; do NOT trace trends — e.g. HDI 2011 vs 2017 base shifts), `comparable_within_state_over_time` (trace one state; do NOT rank states), `directional_only` (read direction-of-change only — e.g. WPI across multiple base splices). v1.0–v1.4 tokens kept valid for back-compat: `comparable_across_states` ≡ `comparable_across_states_and_time`; `not_comparable_across_states` ≡ `directional_only`; `comparable_with_normalisation` should be replaced per-artifact with one of the 4 new tokens on next touch. Renderer behaviour: `directional_only` (and the deprecated `not_comparable_across_states`) suppress rank-table views and label the choropleth as 'illustrative, not a ranking'. Per Governance review 2026-05-11 + Hans plan 2026-05-15.

funding_split.description: Who funds the activity this indicator measures. Critical for centre-vs-state attribution: a citizen reading 'state X is doing well on X' must know whether the state government, the Centre, or a centrally-sponsored scheme drove the outcome. Per Governance review 2026-05-11.

implementing_authority.description: Who actually executes (vs funds) the activity this indicator measures. Many CSS schemes are centre-funded but state-implemented (`joint`); judiciary is `centre`; municipal sanitation is `local_body`. Per Governance review 2026-05-11.

methodology_vintage.description: Free-form short string naming the methodology revision the values were computed under. Examples: 'GSDP base 2011-12', 'Census 2011 + UIDAI 2024 projection', 'NFHS-5 (2019-21) sampling frame', 'UDISE+ post-2018-19 schema'. Distinct from `time` (which is the observation year) — a 2024 NFHS-6 figure has the same time as a 2024 NFHS-5 backcast but a different vintage. Per Governance review 2026-05-11.

chart_type.description: Preferred default primitive for the citizen-facing render. Optional — absent = `choropleth` (the default for single-value-per-entity indicators). `stacked-trend` is for facetted indicators where rows[].facet is populated and the composition over time is the story (e.g. installed capacity by fuel source). `ranked` is for entity-rank tables. The frontend may still choose a different primitive on a specific page; this is the metadata-driven default. Per ADR-0024.

default_mode.description: For stacked-trend and ranked views, whether absolute units or percent-of-total reads more honestly by default. `percent` is the citizen-default for compositional indicators (fuel mix, scheme outlay shares) where the total varies wildly across entities and the share is what's comparable. `absolute` is the default for indicators where the magnitude itself is the story. Per ADR-0024.

facet_labels.description: Optional map from a `rows[].facet` value to its human-readable label (e.g. {"coal": "Coal", "other_thermal": "Other thermal"}). Populated by the composer for facetted indicators so the frontend does not have to carry topic-level hardcoded label literals. When absent, the renderer falls back to humanise(facet_id) (snake_case to Title Case). Per Phase 4 C2 of TODO/VIZ-LAYER-GAPS-PLAN.md.

revision_tier_by_period.description: Optional v1.5 (Hans) field. Declares the upstream revision tier active over a contiguous period of the series — e.g. fiscal data published as Budget Estimate (BE), Revised Estimate (RE), or Actual (A). Distinct from `rows[].vintage` (per-row label): this field is the period-level governance contract that lets the renderer disclose 'recent FY values are still BE; the trend you see WILL revise'. Per Hans plan 2026-05-15.

excludes.description: Optional v1.5 (Hans) field. Citizen-facing what's-NOT-counted bullets the renderer surfaces inline on the indicator card and on its docs page. Examples: 'IGNOAPS / IGNWPS social pensions excluded' (state pension expenditure); 'rural housing not collected by NSO' (housing CPI). Critical for honest reading: a citizen-readable indicator name often implies more than the source actually measures. Per Hans plan 2026-05-15.

renderer_rules.description: Optional v1.5 (Hans) field. Slug-strings from a controlled vocabulary defined in docs/concepts/indicator-naming.md §10.2 that bind renderer behaviour to the indicator's data shape. Live vocabulary today: 'no_rank_table' (suppress rank line), 'no_growth_across_break' (refuse YoY growth crossing a series_break), 'mask_be_in_long_view' (visually distinguish Budget-Estimate periods from Actuals), 'force_per_capita_choropleth' (block raw-magnitude choropleth for a not-comparable raw count). New slugs validate (slug shape only) and are ignored by older renderers — see §10.2 'Adding a new slug' for the 3-place change. Per Hans plan 2026-05-15.

series_breaks.description: Time-points where the series methodology changed in a way that breaks comparability across the boundary. Charts must draw a vertical marker at each break and refuse to compute growth rates that cross one. Per Governance review 2026-05-11.

rows.description: Long-form fact rows. Every row carries the entity id (matching `indicator.entity_kind`), a time stamp at the declared `time_grain`, and a numeric value.

entity_id.description: Identifier matching `indicator.entity_kind` (e.g. "S22" for entity_kind=state, "S22-167" for constituency).

time.description: YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD. Must be consistent with `indicator.time_grain` (validator-side check, not a JSON Schema constraint). Machine sort/join key; the citizen-facing string lives in optional `period_label`.

period_label.description: Optional (v1.6+) adapter-owned citizen label matching the period token from `series_spec.expected_periods[].label` (e.g. 'FY 2024-25', 'as on 31.03.2025', 'Census 2011'). MUST be citizen-readable verbatim — the planner and frontend pass it through, never parse or normalise. When absent, the frontend falls back to `time`.

value.description: Numeric measurement. `null` is allowed and means "not available for this entity-time" (distinct from zero).

facet.description: Optional sub-category for stacked/grouped views (e.g. fuel type for installed capacity: "coal" / "hydro" / "solar"). Null when not faceted.

vintage.description: Optional upstream revision-tier label for the row. Used when the source publishes the same (entity, time, indicator, facet) pair in multiple revision tiers (e.g. MoSPI NAS: 'First Advance Estimates' → 'Second Advance Estimates' → 'Provisional Estimates' → 'First Revised Estimates' → 'Second Revised Estimates' → 'Third Revised Estimates' → 'Final Estimates' → 'Additional Revision'). The ingest picks the most-final tier per row and stamps it here so the chart can disclose 'this year may still revise'.

series_spec.description: v4.0: shrunk to `description` only. The expected_geographies / expected_periods / expected_periods_inference sub-fields moved to the external completeness index (datasets/reference/in/indicators-completeness.json) per ADR-0026. The editorial description of what the series IS stays here, on the citizen-facing artifact.

methodology.description: Optional in v1.6, required in v2.0. Per-indicator definition, publisher, breaks, caveats, and (folded from the old indicator-notes sidecar) related_indicators / editor_note_md / policy_context / chart_defaults.

definition.description: One-paragraph plain-English definition of what this indicator measures.

publisher.description: Issuing authority. e.g. 'Reserve Bank of India'.

methodology_breaks.description: Hand-authored. Empty array = 'no breaks documented yet' (NOT 'no breaks exist').

at.description: Free-text period where the break occurred.

known_caveats.description: Hand-authored. Empty array = 'no caveats documented yet' (NOT 'no caveats exist').

notes.description: Free-form methodological notes. Former .notes.json sidecar fields get their own typed homes below; do not flatten them all here.

divergence.description: Reserved for the divergence-band methodology (Max Roser / OWID-style trajectory bands). Always null in v1.6 / v2.0; the field exists so future ingest adapters can populate it without a schema bump.
